text.title: Structured Logging as Code Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Structured Logging as Code. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: logger.info(f"user {email} did {action}") - PII shipped raw; non-indexable.
antipattern.root-cause: Direct interpolation; no redaction; no JSON.
antipattern.fix: Switch to structured logger with kwargs; pass user_id_hashed through redaction; serialize JSON.

antipattern.symptom: Exception logged with full request body containing card_number.
antipattern.root-cause: Exception handler logged request without scrubbing.
antipattern.fix: Add redaction at the exception serializer; assert in CI parser fixture.

antipattern.symptom: Background worker logs lack trace_id; cross-service debugging impossible.
antipattern.root-cause: OTel context not propagated into worker.
antipattern.fix: Inject trace_id at enqueue time; restore in worker; assert in CI fixture.

antipattern.symptom: DEBUG enabled in prod for 'just one week'; log bill doubles; signal drowns.
antipattern.root-cause: Toggle flipped; sampling not in place.
antipattern.fix: Restore INFO + 1% sampled DEBUG; remove manual toggles; alert on level config drift.
